interview.q: For the tool-agnostic architecture, which approach should we use for tool definitions?
interview.a: Bash associative arrays - keep everything in dev-tools.sh, define tools as arrays with name, command, success_pattern, count_extractor

interview.q: How should dev-tools.sh detect which frontend tools to run for a project?
interview.a: Auto-detect: if package.json exists AND eslint.config.*/tsconfig.json exists, run those tools

interview.q: Where should dev-tools.sh look for configs? (after audit)
interview.a: Standard paths: backend/ then root for Python, frontend/ then root for JS/TS. All projects follow this pattern. monkey-fight needs eslint config added.

interview.q: What's the primary success metric for this refactor?
interview.a: All tools have TOON wrappers + tests pass. Ensure run_X_toon exists for each tool AND existing tests still pass.
